question:
Over $30M committed to the Makina public sale?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if the total USDC commitments for the Makina raise on Legion exceed the number specified in the title before the raise closes. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is the USDC-denominated commitments recorded in the allocation contract at:
https://etherscan.io/address/0xd90537959dfb699d9ca4898082b70fb238c3dc1f

If the final USDC commitment amount cannot be verified by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, or if relevant information becomes permanently unavailable during the market period, this market will resolve to “No.”

evidences.title: Legion raises $5M to disrupt fundraising with onchain solutions. | Legion | Merit-based Fundraising posted on the topic | LinkedIn
evidences.content: Legion has raised $5M to reimagine fundraising as Wall Street moves onchain. Led by VanEck and Brevan Howard Digital with participation from Kraken, Coinbase Ventures, Crypto.com Capital and more. And yes, we’ve saved room for you in the round (on Legion, of course). Over $100T in digital assets, from startup equity to private credit and IP, are migrating onto crypto rails. BlackRock is launching onchain funds. Robinhood is building on Arbitrum. Visa is piloting USDC settlement. TradFi and crypto converge. It’s not just a tech shift. It’s a chance to rewrite who gets funded… and who gets to invest. For decades, capital formation has been rigged for the elite. Now we have the tools to break the old system. Legion is building for the next generation of public offerings, with a roadmap that [...] next generation of public offerings, with a roadmap that includes: - New asset classes like tokenized equity - Bigger and better deals - Mass distribution partners - A full-stack token launch partner (fundraise, CEX listings, market making and GTM advisory, etc) Our mission is simple: build the most trusted brand in onchain capital formation, and bring the most promising deals to public an...

evidences.title: Kraken to host token sales through Legion on its new Launch platform
evidences.content: Asked about the criteria for “highest-profile” sales, Legion co-founder Matt O’Connor told The Block that means raises with “sufficiently high target raise sizes.” Smaller, earlier-stage offerings will continue to run independently on Legion. O’Connor expects “the majority” of Legion sales to also run on Kraken Launch, with a focus on curating high-quality projects rather than chasing volume. The first joint sale will be unveiled within the next week, he added. [...] Discover the institutional crypto exchange LMAX Digital through high-level info and live data here. ✕ Institutional crypto intel Explore platform Legion to host token sales on its new Kraken Launch platform, with Legion aiming to reach a wider user base through the tie-up. Legion, backed by investors including Kraken, will now share select “highest-profile” token sales with the exchange, the firms said Thursday. Tokens from these joint sales will be listed for trading on Kraken shortly after they conclude. evidences.title: Crypto exchange Kraken taps Legion to open token sales to over 15m users - DL News
evidences.content: ICOs got a bad reputation following their prior boom in 2017 and 2018, which saw over 2,000 projects raise some $13 billion. The problem? The speed and low barriers to entry meant ICOs were prime targets for unsavory behavior, with at least $1.3 billion being lost to scams, according to Statis Group. Legion aims to change that with Legion Scores, its criteria for selecting investors. This is meant to weed out bots as well as unscrupulous token buyers. “Legion Scores level the playing field for everyone,” Fabrizio Giabardo, Legion co-founder, said. “The size of your wallet isn’t what matters. What matters is how you’ve contributed to the industry or project itself.” Generic art Tether USDT [...] Hello! This chart will be available in a few moments This time around, the Legion co-founders are hoping to keep the advantages — ultra-fast fundraisers from a global investor group – and exclude the disadvantages — teams using raised funds to sustain their project’s development. ## Fixing market structure As part of the onboarding process to Legion, projects looking to execute a token sale must issue a white paper that is also compliant with Europe’s strict Markets in Crypto-Assets rules...

evidences.title: Analyzing the $4M Makina Finance Exploit - by th13vn
evidences.content: With the `sharePrice` inflated, the attacker interacted with the Makina Machine, depositing/swapping the remaining 110 million USDC against the protocol’s liquidity. Due to the distorted valuation, the protocol credited the attacker with significantly more shares/value than the true market rate. 5. Exit & Profit: The attacker reversed the pool manipulation (or simply withdrew) and paid back the flash loan, pocketing the difference—roughly $4.13M (mostly captured by an MEV builder). ### Root Cause [...] 1. Flash Loan: The attacker borrowed a massive amount of liquidity (approximately 280 million USDC) to secure the capital needed for manipulation. 2. Market Distortion: The attacker deployed roughly 170 million USDC into the DUSD/USDC Curve pool. This unbalanced the pool significantly, distorting the output of `calc_withdraw_one_coin` and the spot price reported by the pool. 3. Oracle Update: Makina’s `MachineShareOracle` updated its internal price based on this manipulated state. Because the protocol relied on the spot liquidity/balance of the Curve pool without sufficient Time-Weighted Average Price (TWAP) or liquidity cap checks, the `sharePrice` was artificially inflated. 4. A...

evidences.title: S-1/A
evidences.content: CCTP works by first redeeming USDC on the source chain and then issuing USDC on the destination chain, relying on a cryptographic attestation, or receipt, to prove that the USDC has been properly redeemed before the corresponding USDC can be reissued on another blockchain. From its launch in April 2023 to December 31, 2024, CCTP has handled approximately $24.7 billion in transfers and is available as a public protocol through a series of smart contracts that work alongside the core USDC stablecoin protocol. While nascent, we view CCTP as an important capability and a unique differentiator that will act as a key driver of our future growth. evidences.title: Kraken to host token sales through Legion on its new Launch platform
evidences.content: Legion’s platform uses a merit-based scoring system, allowing founders to allocate tokens to loyal community members and project-aligned investors. The platform says its token sales are MiCA-compliant in Europe and that it has been in discussions with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission crypto task force as it looks to expand access to U.S. participants as early as Q4. While access to Legion-specific sales will be determined by its scoring system, Kraken Launch offerings will initially open on a first-come, first-served basis. Up to 20% of each sale will be reserved for Legion Score holders, with the rest available publicly on both Kraken and Legion, the firms said. ## 'First ICO underwriter' [...] Legion co-founder Fabrizio Giabardo, however, said the platform ultimately plans to work with multiple exchanges and parties to expand distribution. Kraken, which is reportedly gearing up for a public listing as early as the first quarter of 2026, has rolled out several new products amid a more permissive regulatory environment in the U.S., including a tokenized xStock product in the EU and retail-focused perps trading. Earlier this month, the exchange said it acquired the pro...

This chart will be available in a few moments This time around, the Legion co-founders are hoping to keep the advantages — ultra-fast fundraisers from a global investor group – and exclude the disadvantages — teams using raised funds to sustain their project’s development. ## Fixing market structure As part of the onboarding process to Legion, projects looking to execute a token sale must issue a white paper that is also compliant with Europe’s strict Markets in Crypto-Assets rules, or MiCA. “Our goal is to fix the broken parts of the current market structure, by making the optimal outcome the one where disclosures and transparency are the standard, not the exception,” Giabardo told DL News.
